description: Canonical parity baseline for the Memora adapter layer. Defines the minimum required capabilities that all supported providers must implement or explicitly document as a limitation.

thresholds.description: Advisory hook thresholds. Must be consistent across all providers (FR-008).

entry_contract.description: Each provider adapter must route the agent to the canonical entry surfaces (FR-004).

core_workflows.description: All 9 core memory workflows must be present in each provider adapter (FR-002, FR-003).

claude.notes: memory-explorer is implemented as a sub-agent rather than a skill; behavioral output is equivalent.

codex.notes: Discovery path via .agents/skills/ symlinks pointing to .codex/skills/.

qwen.notes: Qwen maintains dual surface: .qwen/agents/ and .qwen/commands/ must stay in sync.

hooks.description: All 3 advisory hook types must be wired in each provider. Hooks must be non-blocking and advisory-only (FR-005, FR-006, FR-007, FR-008).
hooks.path_resolution: All providers must resolve script paths relative to the repository root, not the session working directory.

codex.mechanism: Single Stop hook command via wrapper script in config.toml
codex.notes: Codex supports only one [hooks.Stop] entry; run-stop-hooks.sh wrapper runs all three checks sequentially. Hook support marked experimental as of Codex CLI v0.114.0.

opencode.mechanism: ES module plugins subscribing to session.idle and tool.execute.after events
opencode.path_resolution: execFileSync git rev-parse --show-toplevel using ctx.cwd; absolute path passed to execSync

guardrails.description: Security baseline for secret/PII protection (FR-009, FR-010, FR-011, FR-012).

claude.notes: Strongest enforcement among providers. Must not be weakened.

codex.compensating_controls: AGENTS.md and workflow files include security guidance; memory-bank/POLICIES/ defines data handling rules.
codex.notes: Hard enforcement not natively supported. Documented limitation per FR-011.

qwen.notes: Partial enforcement via ignore rules. No deny-list equivalent to Claude's permissions.deny.

opencode.compensating_controls: AGENTS.md and workflow files include security guidance; memory-bank/POLICIES/ defines data handling rules.
opencode.notes: Hard enforcement not natively supported. Documented limitation per FR-011.

documentation_surface.description: Documentation must accurately reflect actual capabilities without overclaiming parity (FR-015).

documented_exceptions.description: Codex hooks support is marked experimental (Codex CLI v0.114.0+). Hook format may change.
OpenCode does not support native deny/ignore enforcement. Advisory-only via workflow guidance.
Codex does not support native deny/ignore enforcement. Advisory-only via workflow guidance.
memory-explorer is implemented as a sub-agent (.claude/agents/) rather than a skill (.claude/skills/). Behavioral output is equivalent to other providers.

verification.description: Automated checks that confirm parity baseline is maintained (FR-013).

checks.description: All workflow files listed in core_workflows.provider_surfaces must exist on disk.
All hook config files listed in hooks.provider_config must exist on disk.
Provider entry files must exist and reference canonical surfaces.
Hard-enforcement guardrail files must exist for providers that support them.
OpenCode plugins must use repo-root path resolution (not relative cwd).
